top_categories.workout_context: Granfondo Novi Sad's rolling hills forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Novi Sad's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
62 miles on rolling hills burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
When someone attacks on rolling hills, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Granfondo Novi Sad, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo Novi Sad, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Novi Sad. Train it until it feels boring.
Base fitness for Granfondo Novi Sad. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on rolling hills. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Varied gradients on rolling hills demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,921ft of climbing on rolling hills does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: June in Novi Sad, Serbia can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Granfondo Novi Sad. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Granfondo Novi Sad’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Granfondo Novi Sad. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Granfondo Novi Sad requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on rolling hills to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 62 miles of rolling hills in Novi Sad, Serbia.
